sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: πολλοὺς ἀναιρῶν πολλὰ θερμαίνοι φρένα . [*]
sections.translation: But she who devised this abhorrent deed against her husband , whose children she bore , a burden under her belt , a burden once dear , but now a hateful ill , as it seems : what do you think of her ? Had she been born a seasnake or a viper , I think her very touch without her bite would have caused anyone else to rot ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ὡς ἂν παρῇ μοι μάρτυς ἐν δίκῃ ποτέ ,
sections.translation:
if shamelessness and an immoral disposition could do so . He again takes up the bloody robe
What name shall I give it , however tactful I may be ? A trap for a wild beast ? Or a shroud for a corpse in his bier , [*] wrapped around his feet ? No , rather it is a net : you might call it a hunting net , or robes to entangle a man ’ s feet .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἐξ οὗ [*] τέκνων ἤνεγχ [*] ὑπὸ ζώνην βάρος ,
sections.translation:
This would be the kind of thing a highwayman might posses , who deceives strangers and earns his living by robbery , and with this cunning snare he might kill many men and warm his own heart greatly .
May such a woman not live with me in my house !

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: τοιάδ ἐμοὶ ξύνοικος ἐν δόμοισι μὴ
sections.translation: Before that may the gods grant me to perish childless !

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ [*] μελέων ἔργων :
sections.translation: Alas ! Alas ! Sorrowful work ! You were done in by a wretched death . Alas ! Alas ! And for the survivor also suffering blossoms .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἔδρασεν ἢ οὐκ ἔδρασε ; [*] μαρτυρεῖ δέ μοι
sections.translation:
Did she do the deed or not ? No , this is my witness , dyed by Aegisthus ’ sword . This is a stain of blood that helps time to spoil the many tinctures of embroidered fabric .
Now at last I speak his praises . Now at last I am present to lament him , as I address this web that wrought my father ’ s death .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: πατροκτόνον θ ὕφασμα προσφωνῶν τόδε .
sections.translation: Yet I grieve for the deed and the punishment and for my whole clan . My victory is an unenviable pollution .

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: οὔτις μερόπων ἀσινῆ [*] βίτον
sections.translation: No mortal being shall pass his life unscathed , free from all suffering to the end .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ἒ ἔ , [*] μόχθος [*] δ ὁ μὲν αὐτίχ , ὁ δ ἥξει . [*]
sections.translation: Alas ! Alas ! One tribulation comes today , another tomorrow .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: ἀλλ ὡς ἂν [*] εἰδῆτ , οὐ γὰρ οἶδ ὅπη τελεῖ ,
sections.translation: But since I would have you know , for I do not know how it will end : I think I am a charioteer driving my team far beyond the course . For my ungoverned wits are whirling me away overmastered , and at my heart fear wishes to sing and dance to a tune of wrath .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ᾄδειν ἕτοιμος ἠδ ὑπορχεῖσθαι κότῳ .
sections.translation:
But while I am still in my senses , I proclaim to those who hold me dear and declare that not without justice did I slay my mother , the unclean murderess of my father , and a thing loathed by the gods .
And for the spells that gave me the courage for this deed I count Loxias , the prophet of Pytho ,

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: τὸν πυθόμαντιν Λοξίαν , χρήσαντ ἐμοὶ
sections.translation:
my chief source . It was he who declared that , if I did this thing , I would be acquitted of wrongdoing . But if I refrained — I will not name the penalty ; for no bowshot could reach such a height of anguish .
And now observe me , how armed with this branch and wreath I go as a suppliant , an outcast for the shedding of kindred blood , to the temple set square on the womb of the earth ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ξὺν τῷδε θαλλῷ καὶ στέφει προσίξομαι
sections.translation: the precinct of Loxias , and to the bright fire said to be imperishable . [*] To no other hearth did Loxias bid me turn . And as to the manner in which this evil deed was wrought , I charge all men of Argos in time to come to bear me witness .

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: καὶ μαρτυρεῖν μὲν ὡς [*] ἐπορσύνθη κακὰ
sections.translation: I go forth a wanderer , estranged from this land , leaving this repute behind , in life or death .

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: ἀλλ εὖ γ ἔπραξας , [*] μηδ ἐπιζευχθῇς [*] στόμα
sections.translation: And you have done well . Therefore do not yoke your tongue to an ill-omened speech , nor let your lips give vent to evil forebodings ,

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: φήμῃ πονηρᾷ [*] μηδ ἐπιγλωσσῶ κακά , [*]
sections.translation: since you have freed the whole realm of Argos by lopping off the heads of two serpents with a fortunate stroke .

sections.label: Section 1048
sections.original: ἆ , ἆ . δμωαὶ γυναῖκες αἵδε Γοργόνων δίκην
sections.translation: Ah , ah ! You handmaidens , look at them there : like Gorgons , wrapped in sable garments , entwined with

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: πυκνοῖς δράκουσιν : οὐκέτ ἂν μείναιμ [*] ἐγώ .
sections.translation: swarming snakes ! I can stay no longer .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: τίνες σὲ δόξαι , φίλτατ ἀνθρώπων πατρί ,
sections.translation: What fantasies disturb you , dearest of sons to your father ? Wait , do not be all overcome by fear .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: οὐκ εἰσὶ δόξαι τῶνδε πημάτων ἐμοί :
sections.translation: To me these are no imagined troubles . For there indeed are the hounds of wrath to avenge my mother .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ποταίνιον γὰρ αἷμά σοι χεροῖν ἔτι :
sections.translation: It is that the blood is still fresh on your hands ; this is the cause of the disorder that assails your wits .

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: ἄναξ Ἄπολλον , αἵδε πληθύουσι [*] δή ,
sections.translation: O lord Apollo , look ! Now they come in troops , and from their eyes they drip loathsome blood !

sections.label: Section 1059
sections.original: εἷς σοὶ [*] καθαρμός : Λοξίας [*] δὲ προσθιγὼν
sections.translation: There is one way to cleanse you : the touch of Loxias

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ἐλεύθερόν σε τῶνδε πημάτων κτίσει .
sections.translation: will set you free from this affliction .

sections.label: Section 1061
sections.original: ὑμεῖς μὲν οὐχ ὁρᾶτε τάσδ , ἐγὼ δ ὁρῶ :
sections.translation: You do not see them , but I see them . I am pursued . I can stay no longer . Rushes out

sections.label: Section 1063
sections.original: ἀλλ εὐτυχοίης , καί σ ἐποπτεύων πρόφρων
sections.translation: Then may blessings go with you , and may the god watch benevolently over you and guard you with favorable fortunes !

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ὅδε τοι μελάθροις τοῖς βασιλείοις
sections.translation: Look ! Now again , for the third time , has the tempest of this clan burst on the royal house and run its course . First , at the beginning , came the cruel woes of children slain for food ; next , the fate of a man , a king ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: δεύτερον ἀνδρὸς βασίλεια πάθη :
sections.translation: when the warlord of the Achaeans perished , murdered in his bath . And now , once again , there has come from somewhere a third , a deliverer , or shall I say a doom ?

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: ποῖ δῆτα κρανεῖ , ποῖ καταλήξει
sections.translation: Oh when will it finish its work , when will the fury of calamity , lulled to rest , find an end and cease ?
